title: Payments (Stripe)
lede: Turn iMash into your payment terminal: connect a merchant account once, and every invoice, quote, and job you send becomes instantly payable — with the money settling straight into your bank account.

steps.title: Open the Payments page
steps.content:
This page is your merchant control center — where you connect a payment account, watch transactions come in, and manage payouts to your bank. It's separate from Billing (which is what you pay iMash); Payments is what your customers pay you.

  1. In the left sidebar, scroll to the CRM section and click Payments (or go directly to /settings/payments).
  2. The header reads Payments — Accept invoice payments directly into your bank account.
  3. If a gold TEST MODE tag appears next to the title, payments are simulated: use test cards like 4242 4242 4242 4242 and no real money moves until live mode is enabled.
💡 Don't see Payments in the sidebar? Either the platform admin hasn't enabled merchant payments for your account yet, or (on a sub-account) your main account hasn't granted you Payments Page Access. Contact them — or support@imash.io — to switch it on.

images.alt: Payments page Overview tab with merchant account status
images.caption: The Payments page: status banners at the top, eight tabs below

images.alt: Payments page Overview tab with merchant account status
images.caption: The Payments page: status banners at the top, eight tabs below

steps.title: Read your Merchant Account status
steps.content:
The Overview tab opens first. Its Merchant Account card tells you at a glance whether you can take money:

  • Active (green) — fully verified; charges and payouts are on.
  • Onboarding in progress (gold) — you started connecting but Stripe still needs information.
  • Restricted (red) — something is past due (verification, bank account, business details) and charging or payouts may be paused until you fix it.
  • Disconnected — the account was unlinked; invoices can't be charged.

Below the status you'll see Account ID, Country, Charges enabled, Payouts enabled, Connected since, and Mode (Test or Live). Use the buttons in the card header:

  • Refresh — pulls your latest live status from the payment provider.
  • Disconnect — unlinks the merchant account (asks for confirmation; shown once onboarding is complete).

images.alt: Merchant Account status card with Restricted tag and account details
images.caption: The Merchant Account card: status tag, alerts, and account details

images.alt: Merchant Account status card with Restricted tag and account details
images.caption: The Merchant Account card: status tag, alerts, and account details

steps.title: Connect (or finish connecting) your merchant account
steps.content:
Connecting takes 5–10 minutes and you only do it once. iMash partners with Stripe, so your banking details go straight to the payment provider — the platform never sees or stores them.

  1. If you've never connected: on the Overview tab, click Connect Merchant Account.
  2. If you started before and see an Onboarding incomplete alert (or the red Your merchant account needs urgent attention banner): click Continue onboarding.
  3. The Connect your merchant account window opens right inside iMash. Click Add information and follow the form: business type, business details (website, product description, support phone), your personal verification, and the bank account your payouts will land in.
  4. When you finish, you return to the Payments page and the status refreshes automatically. Click Refresh on the Merchant Account card if you want to re-check.
⚠️ If you close the form halfway, your account sits in Onboarding in progress (or becomes Restricted with "past due" requirements) and you can't accept payments. The red banner lists exactly which items Stripe still needs — click Continue onboarding and complete just those.

images.alt: Connect your merchant account onboarding window
images.caption: Onboarding happens in an embedded window — click Add information to start

images.alt: Connect your merchant account onboarding window
images.caption: Onboarding happens in an embedded window — click Add information to start

steps.title: Know your way around the eight tabs
steps.content:
Everything money-related lives in one row of tabs:

  • Overview — connection status, balance snapshot, and (for main accounts) sub-account payment permissions.
  • Transactions — every payment your customers make, with fees and refund controls.
  • Disputes — chargebacks and the evidence you submit to fight them.
  • Payouts & Bank — your balance, payout schedule, and bank accounts.
  • Account — business info, statement descriptor, and verification documents.
  • Balances — the full balance picture: available vs. pending, plus top-ups if a refund pulled you negative.
  • Documents — tax forms (like US 1099-K), statements, and payout reports.
  • Notifications — time-sensitive merchant alerts (disputes, payout failures, fraud warnings).

Each tab has its own web address (for example /settings/payments/payouts), so links in payment-provider emails land you exactly where action is needed.

images.alt: Payments tabs row: Overview, Transactions, Disputes, Payouts and Bank, Account, Balances, Documents, Notifications
images.caption: The eight Payments tabs

images.alt: Payments tabs row: Overview, Transactions, Disputes, Payouts and Bank, Account, Balances, Documents, Notifications
images.caption: The eight Payments tabs

steps.title: Track every payment in Transactions
steps.content:
1. Click the Transactions tab.
  2. Once your account is active, every payment made against your invoices appears here as customers pay. Click any line for the full breakdown — gross amount, card processing fee, and platform fee — and to issue a refund directly from the payment detail.
  3. Below the payment list, the Linked transactions card matches each payment to the CRM record behind it — click through to the exact invoice or job that was paid.

Until onboarding is finished, this tab shows an Onboarding not complete notice instead of the list — one more reason to finish step 3.

images.alt: Transactions tab of the Payments page
images.caption: The Transactions tab — payments appear here as customers pay your invoices

images.alt: Transactions tab of the Payments page
images.caption: The Transactions tab — payments appear here as customers pay your invoices

steps.title: Manage payouts and bank accounts
steps.content:
1. Click the Payouts & Bank tab.
  2. Here you view your available balance, set your payout schedule (how often money moves to your bank), and manage the bank accounts your funds land in. Payouts typically arrive within 1–2 business days for card payments and 1–3 business days for bank deposits.
  3. Once your account is active, the Overview tab also grows a Balance & Payouts card with two live tiles — Available (ready to pay out per your schedule) and Pending (captured from customers, settling — typically 2 business days for cards) — plus a Recent payouts table showing Amount, Status, Method, and Arrives date.
💡 New payments always sit in Pending briefly before becoming Available — that's normal card settlement, not a problem with your account.

images.alt: Payouts and Bank tab of the Payments page
images.caption: The Payouts & Bank tab — balance, payout schedule, and bank accounts

images.alt: Payouts and Bank tab of the Payments page
images.caption: The Payouts & Bank tab — balance, payout schedule, and bank accounts

steps.title: Respond to disputes before the deadline
steps.content:
1. Click the Disputes tab to see all open and historical chargebacks. Filter by Reason, Status, Amount, Disputed date, or Respond by, and use Export to download the list.
  2. Click into any dispute to upload evidence — receipts, customer communications, proof of delivery — before the Respond by deadline.
⚠️ Disputes have hard deadlines. Miss the Respond by date and the chargeback is usually lost automatically — check the Notifications tab (step 9) so a new dispute never slips past you.

images.alt: Disputes tab with filters and dispute list
images.caption: The Disputes tab — filter, export, and click into a dispute to upload evidence

images.alt: Disputes tab with filters and dispute list
images.caption: The Disputes tab — filter, export, and click into a dispute to upload evidence

steps.title: Update business details, balances, and documents
steps.content:
Three more tabs round out the picture:

  • Account — update your business information, statement descriptor (the text that shows on your customers' card statements), and verification documents. For security, this tab asks you to Sign in to see details first — a quick identity check with the payment provider before sensitive data is shown.
  • Balances — your full balance picture: what's available for payout right now, what's still pending settlement, your payout cadence, and — if a chargeback or refund pulled your balance negative — a top-up option so future payouts can resume.
  • Documents — every document the payments provider has prepared for your account, including 1099-K tax forms (US merchants meeting the IRS reporting threshold), monthly account statements, and payout reports. Click any row to download a PDF or CSV.

images.alt: Account tab with business settings and sign-in prompt
images.caption: The Account tab — a quick sign-in protects your business and bank details

images.alt: Account tab with business settings and sign-in prompt
images.caption: The Account tab — a quick sign-in protects your business and bank details

steps.title: Watch merchant alerts in Notifications
steps.content:
1. Click the Notifications tab.
  2. The Merchant alerts feed queues time-sensitive events so nothing slips by: Dispute filed, Dispute updated, Payout failed, and Fraud warning.
  3. Each row shows the alert Type, Subject, Email delivery status, and When it happened. Click Refresh to pull the latest.

An empty list here is good news — it means no disputes, failed payouts, or fraud flags need your attention.

images.alt: Notifications tab with merchant alerts feed
images.caption: The Notifications tab — disputes, payout failures, and fraud warnings land here

images.alt: Notifications tab with merchant alerts feed
images.caption: The Notifications tab — disputes, payout failures, and fraud warnings land here

steps.title: Control payments for your sub-accounts (main accounts only)
steps.content:
If you run a team, the Sub-accounts card at the bottom of the Overview tab decides how each team member's invoices get paid. Every sub-account starts fully off, with three independent switches per user:

  • Payment Processing — the master kill switch. ON: their invoices are payable (through their own merchant account or yours). OFF: nothing processes for them, period.
  • Payments Page Access — whether they can open this Payments page themselves. OFF hides the page, but their invoices still process through your merchant account if Payment Processing is on.
  • Independent Processor — ON: they connect and use their own merchant account. OFF: their payments route through your merchant account. (Locked until Page Access is on — they can't manage an account they can't see.)

The Routing and Merchant status columns show, per sub-account, where their money flows and whether their own account is connected.

⚠️ Turning on Independent Processor for a sub who hasn't connected their own account yet means their invoices stop processing until they finish connecting — they will not silently fall back to your merchant account.

images.alt: Sub-accounts permissions card with per-user payment toggles
images.caption: The Sub-accounts card — Payment Processing, Page Access, and Independent Processor per user

images.alt: Sub-accounts permissions card with per-user payment toggles
images.caption: The Sub-accounts card — Payment Processing, Page Access, and Independent Processor per user

steps.title: Put it to work: pay links, saved cards, tap-to-pay, and refunds
steps.content:
Once your merchant account is Active, getting paid stops being a chore:

  • Pay links on every invoice and quote — send an invoice by email or use the SMS Text Pay Link option in the send composer, and your customer pays online with a card in seconds. Paid invoices update automatically in your Pipeline.
  • Saved cards — on any lead, contact, or account record, click Add card under Payment Methods to keep a card on file, then charge future invoices in one click — perfect for repeat customers.
  • Tap-to-pay in the field — technicians using the iMash Tech mobile app can take in-person card payments by tapping the customer's card or phone on their own device. No card reader needed.
  • Refunds — open an invoice's payment list (or a transaction in the Transactions tab) and issue a full or partial refund; the payment's status flips to Fully Refunded or Partially Refunded automatically.

That's the whole loop: quote it, invoice it, get paid, and watch the money land in your bank — all without leaving iMash.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content: Stuck on onboarding, a restricted account, or a dispute? Email support@imash.io and we'll help you get paid.
